name: react-doctor
description: Run after making React changes to catch issues early. Use when reviewing code, finishing a feature, or fixing bugs in a React project.
qualityReason: Actionable post-change review and catch issues
capabilities: Scans React code for common mistakes and anti-patterns
Detects performance issues in React components
Identifies missing dependencies in useEffect hooks
Checks for proper React hooks usage
Validates component prop types and prop drilling
author: millionco
securitySummary: Static analysis found two patterns (external_commands, weak cryptographic algorithm) but evaluation confirms these are false positives. The external_commands finding is markdown documentation describing development commands (not actual execution). The weak cryptographic algorithm finding is likely a misidentification of normal text in the markdown file. This is a legitimate React debugging tool with no malicious intent.
valueStatement: This skill automatically detects React code issues after changes, helping developers catch bugs early before they become problems.
limitations: Does not fix issues automatically - only reports them
Cannot detect runtime errors without executing code
May produce false positives in complex codebases

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers working on React projects
useCases.title: Post-Feature Development Check
useCases.description: Run after completing a new feature to catch any React issues before committing

useCases.title: Pre-Review Code Quality Gate
useCases.description: Use as part of code review process to ensure React best practices are followed

useCases.title: Legacy Codebase Audit
useCases.description: Scan older React code to identify technical debt and potential bugs

promptTemplates.title: Basic React Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the react-doctor skill to check my recent React changes for any issues
promptTemplates.scenario: After making code changes in a React project

promptTemplates.title: Component-Specific Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run react-doctor on the Button component and its related files

promptTemplates.title: Full Project Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute react-doctor on the entire src directory to identify all potential issues

promptTemplates.title: Fix Verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Use react-doctor to verify that my bug fix resolved the original issue without introducing new problems

bestPractices: Run react-doctor after every significant code change
Review all warnings, not just errors
Use the skill early in development to prevent issues from compounding
Integrate react-doctor into your CI pipeline for automated checks
antiPatterns: Running react-doctor only when bugs appear (instead of proactively)
Disabling rules without evaluating the trade-offs
Relying solely on react-doctor without manual code review

faq.question: What is react-doctor?
faq.answer: React-doctor is a skill that analyzes React code to identify common mistakes, performance issues, and best practice violations. It helps developers catch problems early.

faq.question: Does react-doctor fix issues automatically?
faq.answer: No, react-doctor only reports issues. It identifies problems and suggests fixes, but you must apply the changes manually.

faq.question: Which React versions does it support?
faq.answer: React-doctor works with React 16.8 and later, supporting both class components and functional components with hooks.

faq.question: Can I customize which rules react-doctor checks?
faq.answer: Configuration options depend on the skill implementation. Check the SKILL.md file for available customization options.

faq.question: Is react-doctor suitable for TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes, react-doctor can analyze TypeScript React projects and may detect additional type-related issues.

faq.question: How often should I run react-doctor?
faq.answer: Run react-doctor after every significant code change, before committing, and as part of your regular code review process.
